text:

Olympic gold medallist Tom Dean is diving into a brand new endeavour, as he's been announced as part of this year's glitteringStrictly Come Dancingline-up. The 24-year-old British swimming hero has decided to take on theBBC dance contest.

Having won three Olympic gold medals, Dean, who trained at the National Centre for Swimming, in Bath, is looking to emulate the success of his mate and fellow pool champ Adam Peaty, 29, who made quite the splash with his partner Katya Jones on Strictly in 2021. Eager to switch from laps to steps, Dean shared his excitement about his latest foray: "Naturally you want to take a break after you've had a full Olympic cycle and the opportunity arose.

"I thought it would be really exciting to try something a bit different. I'll be doing that and straight back into training after it. It's something to look forward to."

READ MORE

Update after major incident which closed part of Bath city centre

Dean is also considering getting some advice from pal Peaty, whose journey on the show was quite the spectacle, saying: "I've not seen [Adam] yet. He did brilliantly on it so I'll be getting a few tips from him, for sure."

Tom is the first celeb to confirm he's strutting onto the 2024 series of Strictly Come Dancing, set to begin in autumn. Despite being at home in the water, he joked about his terrestrial coordination skills, or lack thereof: "I don't do too well out of water in gravity sports.

"I'm throwing myself into it and I know what a hard day's training is like so I'm looking forward to it. I guess we'll find out. It's something to look forward to and once I finish this, back home, relax a bit with the family and then we crack on end of August time. It's exciting."

The revelation of Dean's anticipated appearance on Strictly has emerged among swirling speculation about contestant welfare on the popular BBC dance programme. The BBC is poised to disclose findings from an internal inquiry following accusations from Amanda Abbington, 50, regarding her past dance partner Giovanni Pernice, 33.

Abbington has accused Giovanni of bullying and displaying aggressive behaviour throughout her stint on Strictly in the previous year. However, Pernice firmly denies any accusations of "abusive or threatening behaviour".

After Amanda Abbington recently spoke out on Channel 4 News, the BBC responded with a public statement, mentioning: "Anyone involved in a complaint has a right to confidentiality and fair process and therefore it would be inappropriate to comment further on individuals. However, when issues are raised with us we always take them extremely seriously and have appropriate processes in place to manage this. As we have said before, we would urge people not to indulge in speculation."

The corporation further clarified its position, stating, "More generally, the BBC and BBC Studios takes duty of care extremely seriously. Our processes on Strictly Come Dancing are updated every year, they are kept under constant review and last week we announced additional steps to further strengthen welfare and support on the show."
title: Olympic champion Tom Dean set to join BBC Strictly Come Dancing 2024 line-up - Somerset Live
author: Shania King-Soyza
description: Tom Dean is an Olympic gold medal-winning swimmer
